Name: Vanguard Junrock
Desc: A Common Class Tranquilite TD inhabiting mountainous areas. Often overlooked due to its rock-like appearance.
DescOpen:
A TD constructed with fragmented rocks. It has poor eyesight and a preference for rampaging.

The clinks and clacks of its rock skin colliding with other objects help Vanguard Junrock identify its circumstances.

Name: Stonewall Bracer
Desc: An Elite Class Tranquilite TD found in mountainous areas. They bear huge sizes and inhabit rocks.
DescOpen: A TD constructed from rocks. Slow and clumsy, it possesses horrendous arm strength and prefers throwing stones to attack its enemies.

Name: Tempest Mephis
Desc: An Overlord Class Whisperin TD hidden in Central Plains. It demonstrates combat abilities similar to those of a human.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

A powerful TD that roams the Central Plains. With the aid of thunderstorms, it keeps devouring human sounds of protection. Regarded as a vanguard of the Crownless.

Name: Crownless
Desc: An Overlord Class Whisperin TD found outside Gorges of Spirit. Known to be bellicose, malevolent, and aggressive. Further observation is needed to better picture this creature.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

Crownless is a Whisperin Tacet Discord born outside the Gorges of Spirits along with the eruption of the strange Tacet Field. A vast mixture of human frequencies is detected within its physical form. For instance, the exhilaration and thirst for victory, the fear and resentment of loss... Humanity's long-suppressed barbarism is reified in Crownless and inflected against itself.

This creature is a pawn dispatched by the enemy in disguise, as well as a harbinger of the scourge of war.

Name: Fission Junrock
Desc: A Common Class Tranquilite TD inhabiting mountainous areas. Often overlooked due to its lava-like shape.
DescOpen:
A TD constructed with fragmented rocks. Commonly found in the company of Vanguard Junrock.

Different from the latter, Fission Junrock has a more active and unstable energy and easily explodes under impact. Records show that it is sometimes used as a throwing weapon by other Tacet Discords.

Name: Electro Predator
Desc: A Common Class Whisperin TD that frequents the wilderness. Records show it is capable of pulling bowstrings like humans and shooting lightning as arrows.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

A humanoid Tacet Discord wielding hunting gear that fires lightning arrows.

Electro Predators' string pulls are as fast as lightning strikes in the first of spring. The wastelands are its hunting ground, and it takes delight in the pursuit of prey.

Name: Glacio Predator
Desc: A Common Class WhisperinTD that frequents the wilderness. Records show it is able to gather Glacio energy to form a "projectile" and hurl it like humans.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

A humanoid Tacet Discord wielding ice-tipped hunting gear.

Glacio Predators' spears are as precise as lightning that slices the wind. The wastelands are its hunting ground, and it takes delight in the pursuit of prey.

Name: Aero Predator
Desc: A Common Class Whisperin TD that frequents the wilderness. Records show it is able to gather wind as boomerangs to toss and attack like humans.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

A humanoid TD wielding boomerangs of storm as hunting gear.

Aero Predators' boomerangs are an unpredictable vortex morphing around the seas. The wastelands are its hunting ground, and it takes delight in the pursuit of prey.

Name: Fusion Warrior
Desc: A Common Class Whisperin TD commonly found in the wild. Records show that a sharp blade is grown from its arm.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

A humanoid Tacet Discord with blades grown from its arms.

Yearning for powerful opponents, it burns with a fighting spirit as surging as the blades at the end of its limbs.

Name: Havoc Warrior
Desc: A Common Class WhisperinTD commonly found in the wild. Records show that a giant scythe is grown from its arm.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

A humanoid Tacet Discord with scythes grown from its arms.

It favors less powerful opponents, for only the weak are at the mercy of its trials and sentences.

Name: Snip Snap
Desc: A Common Class Whisperin TD widely distributed in the wild. It can wield a small amount of Fusion energy for attacks.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

An immature humanoid Tacet Discord that exudes small amounts of magma, as though they are on the verge of boiling point.

When they battle, the magma on their body erupts with sparks and emits a crackling sound similar to "snip-snapping."

Name: Zig Zag
Desc: A Common Class Whisperin TD widely distributed in the wild. It can wield a small amount of Spectro energy for attacks.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

An immature humanoid Tacet Discord that often emits a "zig zag" sound when they focus and release light rays.

Name: Whiff Whaff
Desc: A Common Class Whisperin TD widely distributed in the wild. It can wield a small amount of Aero energy for attacks.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

An immature humanoid TD, hovering in the air with the help of winds. It is able to alter its movement speed through the simple absorption of gases.

When it exhales air backwards and accelerates, it often emits a sound akin to "whoosh."

Name: Tick Tack
Desc: A Common Class Whisperin TD widely distributed in the wild. It can wield a small amount of Havoc energy for attacks.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

An immature humanoid Tacet Discord. Maybe imitating the clacking of teeth of human laughter, they always make sounds of "tick tack" whenever they encounter humans.

Name: Gulpuff
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD active in water. It excels at disguising and sneaking attacks on nearby enemies.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

Fish-like animals widely found in bodies of water. Gulpuffs often hide underwater with only the lotus leaf-like structure on their head breaking the surface.

When threatened, these creatures swallow a large amount of water before inflating themselves and emitting gulping sounds to frighten enemies.

Name: Chirpuff
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD widely distributed in water areas. Its size is relatively small.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

Widely distributed riparian fish-like animals with a special algal flower structure on the crown of their skull and capable of drifting in the wind.
When threatened, Chirpuff will rapidly puff air and make a warning chirp.

Name: Glacio Prism
Desc: A Common Class Tranquilite TD often found gathering in the wild. Its shape resembles a frost ore.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

A mineral TD commonly found in mine veins. The crystals embedded in its rock body reflect a cold light.

Although its combat capability is relatively feeble, when it is destroyed, the energy it releases enhances the Tacet Discords in its vicinity.

Name: Fusion Prism
Desc: A Common Class Tranquilite TD often found gathering in the wild. Its shape resembles a lava rock.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

A mineral TD commonly found in mine veins. Under its rock body, blazing magma is contained.

Although its combat capability is relatively feeble, when it is destroyed, the energy it releases enhances the Tacet Discords in its vicinity.

Name: Spectro Prism
Desc: A Common Class Tranquilite TD often found gathering in the wild. Its shape resembles a golden stone.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

A mineral TD commonly found in mine veins. Its rock body emits a blinding, brilliant glow.

Although its combat capability is relatively feeble, when it is destroyed, the energy it releases enhances the Tacet Discords in its vicinity.

Name: Havoc Prism
Desc: A Common Class Tranquilite TD often found gathering in the wild. Its shape resembles a pitch-dark lode.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

A mineral TD commonly found in mine veins. Its rock body is haunted with black and profound powers.

Although its combat capability is relatively feeble, when it is destroyed, the energy it releases enhances the Tacet Discords in its vicinity.

Name: Cruisewing
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD often found floating in the wild. Its power is puny.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

Mayfly-like animals with a light body. They have wings like those of butterflies. Good at circling their enemies with flexible moves.

Name: Sabyr Boar
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD that frequents the wild. It charges at incredible speeds.
DescOpen:
Boar-like animals commonly found in forests, with a sensitive and irritable nature.

Sharp tusks are the solution to every problem a Sabyr Boar encounters – it will always charge towards anything that intrudes its territory unrelentingly.

A Huanglong idiom also compares "Sabyr Boar's charge" to reckless people.

Name: Excarat
Desc: A common Class, terrestrial, Howler TD. Its size dictates its limited power.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

Mole-like animals with excellent burrowing capabilities. They are able to excavate even concrete and steel.

With their complicated underground passage system, Excarats can easily hide their traces and confuse their enemies.

Name: Baby Viridblaze Saurian
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD often sighted in mountainous areas. It usually follows and works in concert with Tacet Discords of larger sizes.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

Large amphibian-like creatures mostly found in forests. Their tough skin emits heat.

Research has found that the toughness of Viridblaze Saurian's skin is positively correlated to its body temperature.

Name: Baby Roseshroom
Desc: A Common Class Tranquilite TD distributed in damp areas. Usually sighted beside maturer individuals.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

Roseshrooms are fungus-like TDs inhabiting wetlands, whose caps are like the petals of a rose when fully bloomed, hence the name.

While they are stationary, the core on their caps can gather and release light rays to attack passing creatures.

Name: Exile
Desc: Common Exiles roaming the wilderness of various nations. Mostly taking actions in groups.
DescOpen:
Non-Resonators of Exile groups. They engage in a series of ordinary tasks such as combat, patrol, and item gathering for the team.

Though they often bow to opponents more powerful than themselves, they are experts at dealing with other Exile groups.

Name: Exile
Desc: Common Exiles roaming the wilderness of various nations. Mostly taking actions in groups.
DescOpen:
Non-Resonators of Exile groups. They engage in a series of ordinary tasks such as combat, patrol, and item gathering for the team.

Though they often bow to opponents more powerful than themselves, they are experts at dealing with other Exile groups.

Name: Hooscamp Flinger
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD living nomadically across Dim Forest. Individuals live in packs and follow a leader. Able to imitate the way human use simple tools.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

Gibbon-like lifeforms primarily gathered in deep woodlands. Hoochiefs tend to show off their supremacy by stealing human belongings.

They are TDs within Huanglong borders found to manifest relatively sophisticated cognitive abilities, capable of emulating humans in the use of certain rudimentary weapons and tools. In this case, flinging rocks.

Name: Diamondclaw
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD living in obscured environments. Records show that it possesses an impressive counterattack ability.
DescOpen: Scorpion-like animals found mostly in dim areas. Their thick, hard crystal claws are deadly weapons against enemies.

Name: Hoartoise
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD often sighted in shallow waters. Judging from the records of charging humans on its own initiative, it might have a rather irritable temper.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

Tortoise-like amphibious creatures mostly found along riverbanks. The shells on their backs are embedded with pieces of frost crystals. These creatures favor cold environments for naps.

A warmer surrounding will wake up a Hoartoise, as well as kindle its hot-tempered nature.

Name: Fusion Dreadmane
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD widely distributed in the wild. Individuals often take actions in groups and follow the leader in charges.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

Wolf-like animals that roam dry areas, known for their fury and bloodlust.

In order to fight against more powerful enemies, Annihilation Dreadmanes usually hunt in packs. Their operations are marked by the art of carnage.

Name: Violet-Feathered Heron
Desc: An Elite Class Howler TD found in forests and shores. Their aggressive nature is often manifested in their thunderous actions.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

Heron-like animals active in mountain forests and water edges. Though born with wide-spreading purple wings, they only extend them and fly in furious thunderstorms.

Name: Cyan-Feathered Heron
Desc: An Elite Class Howler TD found in forests and shores. Their aggressive nature is often shown in their stormy moves.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

Heron-like animals active in mountain forests and water edges. Though born with wide-spreading cyan wings, they only extend them and fly in roaring winds.

Name: Flautist
Desc: An Elite Class Whisperin TD often seen in the wild. Records show it looks like an instrumentalist with a flute in hand.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

A Humanoid TD that casts its power by playing the instrument. Imitating the human behavior of playing flutes to transmit messages, the melody it produces is only noisy and hoarse.

Name: Tambourinist
Desc: An Elite Class Whisperin TD often seen in the wild. Records show it looks like an instrumentalist with a tambourine in hand.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

A Humanoid TD that casts its power by playing the instrument. Imitating the human behaviour of playing bells and saying prayers, the words it emits are never blessings, but curses.

Name: Rocksteady Guardian
Desc: An Elite Class Whisperin TD with a humanoid appearance. With a colossal size like a monolith, it is usually spotted in the wild.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

A tall TD guardian. Its figure resembles monolithic rocks from the mountains.

Despite its appearance of an indestructible fortification, the nature of the Rocksteady Guardian is never to protect, but to crush any obstacle that blocks its advance.

Name: Chasm Guardian
Desc: An Elite Class Whisperin TD with a humanoid appearance. With a colossal size like a fortification, it is usually spotted in the wild.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

A tall TD guardian. Its figure resembles a huge boulder from the abyssal depths.

Despite its appearance of an indestructible fortification, the nature of Chasm Guardian is never to protect, but to crush any obstacle that blocks its advance.

Name: Viridblaze Saurian
Desc:
Large amphibian-like creatures mostly found in forests. Their hard skins are found to emit searing heat.
Research has found that the tenacity of Viridblaze Saurian's skin has a positive correlation to the beast's body temperature.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

Large amphibian-like creatures mostly found in forests. Their tough skin emits heat.

Research has found that the toughness of Viridblaze Saurian's skin is positively correlated to its body temperature.

Name: Roseshroom
Desc: An Elite Class Tranquilite TD distributed in the wetlands. No records have documented voluntary moving attempts of this TD.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

Roseshrooms are fungus-like creatures inhabiting wetlands, whose caps are like the petals of a rose when fully bloomed, hence the name.

While they are stationary, the core on their caps can gather and release light rays to attack passing creatures.

Name: Havoc Dreadmane
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD widely distributed in the wild. Individuals often take actions in groups and this particular TD is often found to lead the charges.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.
Wolf-like animals that roam the plains, known for their opportunistic nature.

In order to fight against more powerful enemies, Havoc Dreadmanes usually hunt in packs. Their operations are marked by the art of ambush.

Name: Hoochief Cyclone
Desc: An Elite Class Howler TD living nomadically across Dim Forest. Individuals live in packs. Records show that this TD can bend the power of the wind to their will and attack with it.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

Gibbon-like lifeforms primarily gathered in deep woodlands. Hoochiefs tend to show off their supremacy by stealing human belongings.

Unlike other Hoochiefs, Hoochief Cyclones can conjure air currents to not only launch long-range attacks but also restrict the movement of their targets.

Name: Spearback
Desc: An Elite Class Howler TD inhabiting the forest. It is often found on high alert.
DescOpen: Ferocious black bear-like animals. Their head and shoulders are covered with arrow-shaped clusters of sharp Tacetite, like indestructible thorns.

Name: Carapace
Desc: An Elite Class Howler TD hiding itself in city ruins. Records show that its special camouflage methods present great troubles for people looking to identify it.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

TDs living in abandoned vehicles. It camouflages itself among ruins and foliage. Once disturbed, they exhibit terrifying behaviors.

Name: Exile Leader
Desc: Leaders of the Exiles, usually found in the Exile Campgrounds. They have more combat experience and skills than the average Exiles.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

Leaders of Exile groups who oversee the allocation of resources and the arrangement of operations.

Most Exiles adhere to the law of survival of the fittest, thus Exile Leaders are often chosen through numerous intense battles.

Name: Exile Technician
Desc: Technician members of the Exiles, commonly found in Exile Campgrounds. They are in charge of the repairing and making of weapons and equipment.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

Female Resonators of Exile groups. As mechanics, Exile Technicians are in charge of making and repairing weapons and other equipment.

Effective craftwork can significantly boost the group's survival probability, while deadly weapons can guarantee wins for the group in battle for resources

Name: Fractsidus Executioner
Desc: Loyal believers in the Fractsidus lurking in the shadows.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

Fractsidus' faithful cultists.

Lacking in Forte yet bearing a strong yearning for power, they crossed forbidden scientific boundaries. Through the use of special mechanical components, their human bodies are amalgamated with TD limbs, thus they obtain the ability to render themselves invisible.

However, TD frequencies distort the cultists' mentality and corrode their bodies. Executioners are tasked with the removal of the Fractsidus members in breach of rules or gone out of control.

Name: Inferno Rider
Desc: An Overlord Class Whisperin TD located in the Port City of Guixu. The only existing records noted a unique movement method of it among other Tacet Discords.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

A fiery "biker" born in the Port City of Guixu. It sets forth trials of courage with its unyielding flame for those who dare to trespass.

Throughout the long years, the truth of history gradually burns to ashes, and the knight who once sang boldly toward death becomes a harbinger of fear in the word of mouth among humanity.
"That is the rider who turns fear into fuel and brings calamity."

However, amidst the idle talk of the indigenous people in the Port City of Guixu, another version quietly spreads.
"When you face that mighty rider, set aside your fear and respond with your utmost reverence. Prove to him your courage to be the last one standing."

Name: Impermanence Heron
Desc: An Overlord Class Howler TD showing up in Desorock Highland. It is known to be ferocious and highly aggresive.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

A giant avian creature that flies around Desorock Highland. Its three heads are respectively responsible for feeding, offense, and defense.
"Foolish left, wicked right. Center head's the greatest blight," so goes the Jinzhou rhymes.

Name: Lampylumen Myriad
Desc: An Overlord Class Howler TD lying dormant under Tiger's Maw. It lures preys deep into the mist it hides in before capturing them.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

An ensnarer that slumbers in a freezing mine pit, obsessed with collecting and imitating sounds. It can imitate human speech through a vibrating hum.

Lampylumen Myriad uses sound as bait to lure its prey, which will be frozen in the deep cold. The loss of warmth and the illusion before dying will cause human beings to pour out their last words, which contain great emotional fluctuations.

For Lampylumen Myriad, this is the frequency of the supreme taste.

Name: Feilian Beringal
Desc: An Overlord Class Howler TD in Dim Forest. With a giant size, it boasts the sovereign of this land.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

A giant ape-like monster that resides at the towering Central Banyan, guarding the apes' territory from intrusion.

To avoid unexpected confrontation with it, the Midnight Rangers and the Huaxu Academy once collaborated in a special operation to tag the Feilian Beringal with a tracking bracelet, enabling the tracking of its movements within the forest.

Name: Mourning Aix
Desc: An Overlord Class Howler TD hovering over Whining Aix's Mire. It soars the skies while singing elegies, and attacks fiercely any enemy that comes close.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

A bird-of-prey-like animal that perches at the Whining Aix's Mire, with cries like a mournful song.

Legend has it that this bird once had a partner and that their chorus was a beautiful sound. Their names were not passed down, but their hymns were recorded and preserved by human beings.

Until one of them was deceased. The surviving one cried all day long, and the cries were so dismal it was called the Mourning Aix.

In the loss of a lover, the bird became oppressive and violent, allowing no one to step into its nest of lost love. It only lingers in the heavens while singing the eulogy, in pursuit of an illusion that is no longer there among the clouds.

Name: Bell-Borne Geochelone
Desc: A Calamity Class Howler TD lying dormant in Gorges of Spirit. Due to its permanent inactivity, records of its behaviors are scarce.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

A torpid snapping turtle in the frozen lake, carrying an ancient bell on its back.

Some researchers once tried to transcribe the inscriptions on the bell, but accidentally disturbed the gargantuan Chelonian's dreams.

When it awakens, a drawn-out toll will echo throughout the Gorges of Spirits.

Name: Scar: Aberrant Nightmare
Desc: A special form of one of the Fractsidus Overseers. Such monstrosity wields pitch-black scythe that cleaves through order and law.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG while in human form.
When enraged, he transforms into a grim goat reaper with a higher RES to Spectro DMG and Havoc DMG.

One of the Fractsidus Overseers.

A rebel who revels in Overclocking, disregarding the ideas of beauty and order. He enjoys destruction and chaos, delighting in the suffering of others. He disregards rules and fame, using any means to reach his goals. With an introspective sobriety driving him, he seeks you out to witness the birth of a new world, still in its infancy.

"New life blooms from scorched earth, rising through the ashes."

Name: Hoochief Menace
Desc: An Elite Class Howler TD living nomadically across Dim Forest. Individuals live in packs. Records show that this TD is fractious and prone to stealing human objects.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

Mimetic apes that appear in areas of dense forest seem to make a statement by stealing human objects as trophies.

The Hoochief Menace is particularly manic compared to other mimic apes, and its fists can unleash a blistering barrage of attacks.

Name: Fractsidus Thruster
Desc: Devoted followers of the Fractsidus, frequently collaborating with fellow Artificers. They employ massive hammers to repel adversaries.
DescOpen:
The Fractsidus' faithful cultists.

Lacking in Forte yet bearing a strong yearning for power, they crossed forbidden scientific boundaries. Through the use of special mechanical components, their human bodies are amalgamated with TD limbs, thus they become strong enough to wield gigantic hammers.

However, TD frequencies distort the cultists' mentality and corrode their bodies. They choose to mask themselves to conceal their twisted face.

Name: Hooscamp Clapperclaw
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD living nomadically across Dim Forest. Individuals live in packs and follow a leader. They exhibit a strong territorial tendency.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

An anthropomorphic ape that appears in densely forested areas and seems to make a statement by stealing human items as trophies.

It's one of the more complex TDs with intelligent behavior in Huanglong territory so far, and will use sharp claws to guard its territory.

Name: Fractsidus Cannoneer
Desc: Devoted followers of the Fractsidus, frequently collaborating with fellow Artificers. They can easily wield giant cannons.
DescOpen:
Fractsidus' faithful cultists.

Lacking in Forte yet bearing a strong yearning for power, they crossed forbidden scientific boundaries. Through the use of special mechanical components, their human bodies are amalgamated with TD limbs, thus their figures are unnaturally heightened to shoulder cannons.

However, TD frequencies distort the cultists' mentality and corrode their bodies. They choose to mask themselves to conceal their twisted face.

Name: Fractsidus Gunmaster
Desc: Devoted followers of the Fractsidus, frequently collaborating with fellow Artificers. They fight with guns fused to their limbs.
DescOpen:
Fractsidus' faithful cultists.

Lacking in Forte yet bearing a strong yearning for power, they crossed forbidden scientific boundaries. Through the use of special mechanical components, their human bodies are amalgamated with TD limbs, thus their bodies mutate into powerful guns.

However, TD frequencies distort the cultists' mentality and corrode their bodies. They choose to bandage themselves to conceal their twisted face.

Name: Traffic Illuminator
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD native to the Port City of Guixu. Records show it resembles objects from the city's lost civilization.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

This small enemy, fused with a traffic light, uses the common rule of "stop on red, go on green" to punish those who break traffic rules.

So, please remember to follow the traffic regulations!

Name: Clang Bang
Desc: A Common Class Whisperin TD widely distributed in the wild. Its small body is as fragile as ice.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

An immature humanoid TD. Small amounts of ice crystals are left on its body. It uses them to imitate the crisp sound of “clang bang” of ice colliding.

Name: Autopuppet Scout
Desc: An Elite Class Howler TD that hides itself in the Port City of Guixu. It disguises itself as an abandoned armored vehicle.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

This TD is a parasite of the Sentry Vehicle, camouflaging itself with a rusted and damaged armor shell. It serves as a "sentinel," monitoring all movements within the city and removing any potential threats.

Name: Mech Abomination
Desc: A Staticoid TD that hides beneath an abandoned CSC facility, its physical form comprises a conglomerate of various objects. Threat level: Overlord Class.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

Created in the CSC labs, this Mechanical TD has evolved by consuming machine wrecks.

Upon discovering traces of human frequencies, it became fixated on achieving perfection and grew larger as it improved its composition.

A haunting symbol of humanity's relentless pursuit of scientific advancement, its presence serves as a subtle reminder of our obsession and descent into madness.

Name: Dreamless
Desc: A Whisperin TD discovered in the Norfall Barrens, exhibiting more prominent characteristics of the human female form. There is a suspected link to Threnodian, surrounded by other TD sub-class aggregates. Due to its hazardous environment, there remains insufficient information and records, it has been classified as a Calamity Class.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

Along with the eerie Void Plenilune, this Whisperin TD appeared at the end of the Norfall Barrens.

Its very presence is an ominous reminder of the devastating war that once ravaged this land. Born from the residual energies of battle, it continues to evolve and has become the most feared weapon in the Threnodian's arsenal. With merciless destruction, it reawakens humanity's primal fear of warfare. And now, it greets its ruler, ready to unleash chaos upon the world.

Name: Thundering Mephis
Desc: An Overlord Class Whisperin TD found in Desorock Highland. It demonstrates combat abilities similar to those of a human.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

A powerful TD based in Desorock Highland with the power of thunder. It is considered one of the vanguards in the Threnodian's army.

Name: Lava Larva
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD. It inhabits areas beneath the tundra and is skilled in digging extensive underground tunnels.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

Larvae-mimic TDs mainly inhabit areas beneath the tundra, near geothermal heat sources.

By absorbing geothermal energy, they produce lava through a special organ to melt the frozen soil and dig tunnels extending in all directions.

Lava Larva traces often lead to locations rich in geothermal energy.

Name: Dwarf Cassowary
Desc: A Common Class Howler TD. Despite its wings, it is entirely terrestrial and highly sensitive to the cold.
DescOpen:
An endemic ratite-mimic TD inhabiting Mt. Firmament. Being flightless and non-hardy, it can only survive in areas below the mountain ridge.

Its name has two etymological origins within the Huanglong language family. One stems from a description from the Hongzhen Chronicle: "This ratite-mmic TD is plump and seemingly lacks intelligence."

The other is originated from the local dialect. Locals use the word to describe creatures that "look dumb."

Name: Glacio Dreadmane
Desc: An Elite Class Howler TD widely distributed in the snowland. Glacio Dreadmanes are social TDs that usually move in packs.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

A wolf-mimic TD rampant in the snowland, gifted in stealth.
Glacio Dreadmanes utilize teamwork and mimicry as weapons against powerful opponents.

Name: Lumiscale Construct
Desc: A mechanical construct guarding the ruins in Mt. Firmament. It takes direct orders from the Sentinel and functions as their guardian.
DescOpen:
The enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

Lumiscale Constructs are composed of Jué's defensive modules, commonly known as "Loong's Scales."

They are found throughout the Court of Savantae ruins in Mt. Firmament, tasked with eliminating intruders and potential threats to Jué.

Name: Lightcrusher
Desc: An Elite Class Howler TD. It is solitary and leaves behind luminous traces as it moves.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

An enormous feline-mimic TD, only sighted in Mt. Firmament in Huanglong.

According to the Hongzhen Chronicle, it is a solitary TD with four claws and a scaly back. It is called "Lightcrusher" because of the glowing tracks left in its wake.

Name: Jué
Desc: One of the Sentinels in Huanglong that takes the shape of Loong. With the ability to manipulate time, Jué guides the development of Jinzhou's civilization.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG, and a higher RES to Electro DMG.

One of the Sentinels of Huanglong, taking the shape of a Loong. The guiding force behind Jinzhou's civilization.

A majestic "deity" with unquestionable power, omniscient of Jinzhou's past, present, and future. Jué, the wielder of time's flow, sees eons as mere moments. In an instant, Jué can change the universe forever.

"Seasons shift by decree, and all beings abide by their laws. Such is the nature of the universe."

Name: Fallacy of No Return
Desc: The last line of defense built by the Tethys System, patrolling the borders between reality and digital world.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

It is the last line of defense built by the Tethys System, patrolling the borders between reality and the digital world.

The absolute safety and stability of this system must be preserved at all costs, even if it means exhausting the computing power of the stars, or seizing the power to modulate—

This is the core logic of the system, the foundation to continue the observation, and the unwavering protocol of Tethys.

Name: Scar: Lightbane Reversal
Desc: One of the Fractsidus Overseers. His blazing inferno engulfs all order and rules, leaving chaos in its wake.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG while in human form.
When enraged, he transforms into a grim goat reaper with a higher RES to Spectro DMG and Havoc DMG.

One of the Fractsidus Overseers.

A rebel who revels in Overclocking, disregarding the ideas of beauty and order. He enjoys destruction and chaos, delighting in the suffering of others. He disregards rules and fame, using any means to reach his goals. With an introspective sobriety driving him, he seeks you out to witness the birth of a new world, still in its infancy.

"New life blooms from scorched earth, rising through the ashes."

Once a remnant creature in Nimbus Sanctum, it decayed into a Tacet Discord as its frequency wanes, leaving it wandering in perpetual sorrow.

DescOpen: An incorporeal Tacet Discord inhabiting the body of a sturdy, diligent plushie. He lives on trust and gratitude earned by his hardworking hands—frequencies he finds most delectable.

DescOpen:

A deceptive Tacet Discord disguised as a supply chest, preying on the greed of its victims and devouring those who approach. To this day, no scholar has been able to determine just how much a Chest Mimic can consume. Some records suggest that it has a strange tendency to swallow others of its kind, absorbing the treasures they have claimed. Hence, it's often referred to as the "Rinascita Nesting Doll" in the Pioneer Association's Ecoacoustics reports.

Name: Questless Knight

A humanoid Tacet Discord clad in fine attire, wielding a sharp blade and extraordinary might existing only in legends.

Said to be the lost souls of fallen heroes, it was once witnessed slaying other Tacet Discords to protect villagers. Yet such acts were driven purely by an instinctual hunger for battle.

Though it has lost its heart, it stubbornly clings to the vestiges of past honor, wandering the world in solitary defiance.

Said to be the lost souls of fallen heroes, it retains an air of commanding dignity, even as a Tacet Discord.

Once a knight who has long forgotten the title they once bore, it has lost all it was sworn to protect. Now, only an unyielding obsession with honor remains.

Glorious knights who have long forgotten their titles. Protectors with nothing to defend, bound by their relentless obsession with honor.

A humanoid Tacet Discord born from the abyssal sea that assumes the semblance of an aristocrat, a fully differentiated form of Primordia Bloom.

In its earliest state, the Abyssal Patricius was no different from a Primordia Bloom, a creature capable of consuming others' frequencies to mimic their forms and seamlessly blend with its prey's kind.

Perhaps it originated from an unfortunate noble who tried to return to Thorncrown Rises, only to become sustenance for countless nematocysts.

"Behold, only the fall of a noble can birth another," the Fisalias would say.

Desc: An incorporeal Tacet Discord inhabiting the body of a large, ragged stuffed toy.
DescOpen:
An incorporeal Tacet Discord inhabiting the body of a large, ragged stuffed toy.

He feasts on fear and screams provoked by his intimidating figure—frequencies he finds most delectable. Yet more often than not, what reaches his ears is, "Look at that dirty stuffed toy. So pitiful..."

Is he not fearsome enough? Are such pity-filled frequencies truly edible?

Once a remnant creature in Nimbus Sanctum, Lorelei gained intelligence through Sentinel Imperator's power, entrusted to purify corrupted frequencies drifting in the clouds.

Yet wicked desires have transformed her into the "Queen of the Night," seeking to cloak the Sanctum in endless darkness to clear all sins.

With her final grasp on reason, Lorelei sealed herself within the atrium, where she sings a haunting lament, awaiting one who can awaken her from this dismal slumber.

Any trace of its original form has faded, leaving only finely honed combat instincts within its frequencies. As an unyielding guardian, it adapts seamlessly to all types of combat. When needed, it activates a unique component to enhance mobility or unleashes all its energy in a devastating blast.

The Montelli Family, voyaging from distant shores, has seen a wider world—and they are willing to pay any price to gain the strength needed to revive their homeland.

Once a sacred place, this island is now a grave of sorrow, and anyone who comes for redemption must face its fire of judgment.

Such is Gehinom, the Dragon of Dirge, whose flames burn to the bone, whose pain echoes, yearning for a final release.

Kerasaurs are not indigenous to Septimont. They were introduced as gladiatorial opponents in matches for their distinct appearance and aggressive behavior.

Their pronounced territorial instincts also make them the ideal Echoes to serve in the city's defense force once tamed.

The variation of melodies lends to a symphony of tearing pain, while distorted frequencies weave a frozen cage of imprisonment.

Desc: Inspectors of the Fractsidus who wear the tendrils of darkness emanating from their highly modified body like a badge of honor.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

Fractsidus Inspectors whose highly modified bodies embody their devotion to their zealous ideals.

Shreds of reason linger beneath the cold metal. Through the cracks, all that remains to be seen is the dark void.

Name: Fenrico
Desc: Their grace and divine prophecy have descended, and the faceless Harbinger now stands before the gate.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

A despair devotee, a fallen soul devoured by an illusory paradise.

When the mask of Dark Tide was dropped, he found clarity at the end of his path—hope and redemption traded with a perished form.

He will forever sleep at the bottom of the dark sea, for there too lies the way home.

Born from the utopia of Unity envisioned by the Threnodian, it is a shapeless mass of black sludge given form, a vessel created solely to devour. All things are to unite within the False Sovereign's body and be reborn as servants and children of Leviathan.

It does not think. It does not feel. Only the rippling sludge murmurs its false hymns, eternally glorifying the power of Leviathan.

DescOpen:

She guards the City of Glory, a valiant lioness of the arena.

Built with the pristine white stones that Gryphons are carved out of and tempered in the echoes of battle cries and the laughter of heroes.

This is Arsinosa, ever steadfast, watching over Septimont’s glory for all time.

Appearing alongside the Dark Tide, the Corrosaurus's roar declares the start of a glorious hunt to the Gladiators of Septimont.

DescOpen:

She pierced her own heart and fell into the ocean. But fate, relentless as the whirlpools below, dragged her even deeper.

Now, her creator has claimed this failed vessel, lying in wait beneath the waves to devour the entire world.

Name: Gang Member
Desc: A member of the Ragunna underground gang.
DescOpen:
A member of Ragunna's underground gang.

During a chaotic period in Ragunna's history, many Ragunnesi were forced to gang activities. Some out of survival, others for protection. Masks that once symbolized revelry and celebration were used as tools of concealment, hiding their faces as they carried out acts of violence.

Even today, the few remaining members still cling to the tradition, as though it may help certain people recall the bygone age—the so-called "golden era" when they reveled in lawlessness, and roamed free without fear of consequence.

Name: Hecate
Desc: A special Tacet Discord summoned by Phrolova from a Sonoro Sphere where all decaying frequencies converge, known as the "Beyond." It manifests as a three-faced Witch of the Underworld.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

A special Tacet Discord summoned by Phrolova from a Sonoro Sphere where all decaying frequencies converge, known as the "Beyond." It manifests as a three-faced Witch of the Underworld.

Mortals are nothing more than captives of fate, their struggles mere fleeting illusions. As the phases of the moon wax and wane, only death and oblivion remain unchanged.

On a stage woven from crimson Lycorises, she performs in harmony with Phrolova's orchestration, together crafting a "perfect" symphony of despair.

Name: Fallacy of No Return
Desc: The last line of defense built by the Tethys System, patrolling the borders between reality and digital world.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.

It is the last line of defense built by the Tethys System, patrolling the borders between reality and the digital world.

The absolute safety and stability of this system must be preserved at all costs, even if it means exhausting the computing power of the stars, or seizing the power to modulate—

This is the core logic of the system, the foundation to continue the observation, and the unwavering protocol of Tethys.

Name: Tremor Warrior
Desc: A bent-over humanoid Tacet Discord with electric currents coursing through its veins and a whip-like appendage on its head.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

A bent-over humanoid Tacet Discord with electric currents coursing through its veins and a whip-like appendage on its head.

Observers note that its segmented whip can suddenly release high-voltage electricity. The whip's tip is highly responsive to sensory stimuli, allowing this TD to lock on and restrain targets in the blink of an eye. It primarily attacks with fast-moving claws and sustained whip strikes.

Name: Flora Drone
Desc: A mechanical bumblebee, grown from the Exostrider's own parts. The Roya often use them as tools to collect pollen and plant nectar.
DescOpen: After scanning the data of past animals in Lahai-Roi, the Exostrider used its own parts to grow and proliferate mechanical bumblebees. Due to the drone's small size and uniquely formed body, the Roya often use them as tools to collect pollen and plant nectar.

Name: Mining Drone
Desc: A mechanical bumblebee, grown from the Exostrider's own parts. The Roya often use them as tools to collect fragile minerals from cramped spaces.
DescOpen: After scanning the data of past animals in Lahai-Roi, the Exostrider used its own parts to grow and proliferate mechanical bumblebees. Due to the drone's small size and uniquely formed body, the Roya often use them as tools to collect fragile minerals from tight spaces.

Name: Geospider S4
Desc: An exploration robot developed by the Spacetrek Collective. Agile and nimble, it perfectly suits Lahai-Roi's rugged terrain.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG .

To explore Lahai-Roi with greater efficiency, the Research Institute deployed Geospider robots. Now in its S4 iteration, the Geospider's mobility and intel-gathering capabilities are unmatched. They are frequently taken on scientific expeditions and equipped with a high-precision sensor suite to defend both themselves and personnel.

Name: Zip Zap
Desc: A type of ironclad electromagnetic Murmurin TD that attracts metal to form a protective shell around itself. Electric arcs are faintly visible through cracks in its metallic armor.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

Zip Zap is a type of ironclad electromagnetic Murmurin TD that attracts metal to form a protective shell around itself. Electric arcs are faintly visible through cracks in its metallic armor.

They may attach themselves to other Clamorling Tacet Discords to provide power amplification. When spent, Zip Zaps reverse the flow of their electricity to recharge themselves.

People in Lahai-Roi often catch Zip Zaps in the wild as an emergency power source.

Name: Twin Nova: Nebulous Cannon
Desc: A mech developed by the Spacetrek Collective, which comes in melee and ranged variants. The two models fight gracefully in unison when paired.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG .

Twin Nova: Nebulous Cannon sports a top-of-the-line Combat Synergy Module. Combined with Twin Nova: Collapsar Blade, their battle synergy turns into a deadly dance of multi-pronged attacks.

When Twin Nova: Nebulous Cannon is hit by Tune Break, it takes 20% more total DMG for 30s, stacking up to 3 times.

The Twin Nova mechs were built for exploration beyond the Stridergate. Nebulous Cannon is the ranged model. Their humanoid form embodies mankind's drive to explore the abyss when they look up at the stars.

Together as one, paired Twin Novas demonstrate unprecedented combat capabilities.

Name: Twin Nova: Collapsar Blade
Desc: A mech developed by the Spacetrek Collective, which comes in melee and ranged variants. The two models fight gracefully in unison when paired.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG .

Twin Nova: Collapsar Blade sports a top-of-the-line Combat Synergy Module. Combined with Twin Nova: Nebulous Cannon, their battle synergy turns into a deadly dance of multi-pronged attacks

When Twin Nova: Collapsar Blade is hit by Tune Break, it takes 20% more total DMG for 30s, stacking up to 3 times.

The Twin Nova mechs were built for exploration beyond the Stridergate. Collapsar Blade is the melee model. Their humanoid form embodies mankind's drive to explore the abyss when they look up at the stars.

Together as one, paired Twin Novas demonstrate unprecedented combat capabilities.

Name: Sabercat Prowler
Desc: A machine capable of camouflage. It possesses heightened senses and uses the detector on its tail to track enemy movements.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

When Sabercat Prowler is in a state of Invisibility, its all All-Attribute RES is increased by 30%.

Hitting Sabercat Prowler with Tune Break forces it out of Invisibility.

When Sabercat Prowler is under Tune Rupture - Shifting and hit by Tune Break, it takes additional DMG equal to 25% of its Max HP, triggered only once.

A machine capable of camouflage, its parts are badly corrupted by Voidmatter, causing severe degradation to its vision and hearing. Yet this also heightens its other sensory perceptions. It tracks enemy movements by using the detector on its tail to sense changes in frequency.

Name: Frostbite Coleoid
Desc: A parasitic creature that lives inside Whisperin Tacet Discords. It uses the host's body as a cocoon and feeds on its frequency.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

Frostbite Coleoid is immune to Glacio Chafe, but takes 80% more total DMG taken from other Negative Statuses and Echo Skill.

A parasitic creature that lives inside Whisperin Tacet Discords, using the host's body as a cocoon and feeds on its frequency. Infection by Frostbite Coleoids causes combative behavior and a compulsion to feed on the frequencies of other Tacet Discords. After an incubation period, the Frostbite Coleoid abandons its host by breaking free from the host's back.

Name: Windlash Coleoid
Desc: A parasitic creature that lives inside Whisperin Tacet Discords. It uses the host's body as a cocoon and feeds on its frequency.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG .

The Windlash Coleoid is immune to Aero Erosion, but takes 80% more total DMG taken from other Negative Statuses and Echo Skill.

A parasitic creature that lives inside Whisperin Tacet Discords, using the host's body as a cocoon and feeds on its frequency. Infection by Windlash Coleoids causes combative behavior and a compulsion to feed on the frequencies of other Tacet Discords. After an incubation period, the Windlash Coleoid abandons its host by breaking free from the host's back.

Name: Hyvatia
Desc: The Spacetrek Collective's first detection array built at Sundermere to monitor and stabilize the Reactor Drive's energy state in real time.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG .

When Hyvatia opens its Eye of Prediction, it observes and records Resonators' Dodges and Counterattacks. After the Eye closes, Hyvatia's next attack cannot be Dodged or Counterattacked.

Hyvatia takes 100% more total Tune Rupture DMG. When it's under Tune Rupture - Shifting and hit by Tune Break, it takes 15% more total DMG for 15s.

Hyvatia is Spacetrek Collective's first detection array built at Sundermere to monitor and stabilize the Reactor Drive's energy state in real time.

The Research Institute implanted the Exostrider's space-time error correction module runtime into Hyvatia's core, allowing Hyvatia to adjust the Exostrider's orbit and detect hidden spaceborne threats with high precision. Therefore, Hyvatia possesses sufficient computational power to predict the Reactor Drive's rotation.

Hyvatia's core function is to detect abnormal or dangerous energy fluctuations, perform immediate emergency calibrations, and feed this data back to the Drive to ensure stable operation.

Name: Reactor Husk
Desc: A Reactor Drive component. When the Drive's immune response is triggered, its battle logic will activate to eliminate all intruders.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

Reactor Husk's special ranged attack, Cinder Cut, can be deflected back, dealing damage to the Reactor Husk in return and reducing its Vibration Strength.

After attacking or taking Counterattacks several times, a Vulnerable Core will be exposed on Reactor Husk's left arm. Attacking the exposed core will destroy Reactor Husk's armor, rendering it unable to attack.

Inflicting Negative Statuses on Reactor Husk immediately exposes the Vulnerable Core. If Reactor Husk's armor is destroyed while it is inflicted with Negative Statuses, then it takes 100% more total DMG from Negative Statuses for 30s. During this effect's period, the core will no longer be exposed.

After the Reactor Drive was set above Lahai-Roi by the Exostrider, it remained on high alert to the activities of Aleph-1.

When the Drive's immune response is triggered, a mechanized "Reactor Husk" will arise from the depths of the energy well, with the sole purpose of eliminating intruders at all costs.

Research by Spacetrek Collective shows that Reactor Husk was once part of the Exostrider. Both share a mission to resist the Threnodian threat and safeguard civilization.

Name: Outcast Executioner
Desc: Savage and brutal Roya who broke from tradition and were cast out of the tribe.
DescOpen: The Roya who were cast out of their tribe for selling Exostrider cell remnants. They, however, view themselves as unfettered by the old ways. To mock tradition, they wear masks made from Exoswarm heads and use the Shepherd's Staff as a weapon, and thus become a force of unrestrained chaos in Lahai-Roi.

Name: Thundstaff Outcast
Desc: Savage and brutal Roya who broke from tradition and were cast out of the tribe.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

The Roya who were cast out of their tribe for selling Exostrider cell remnants. They, however, view themselves as unfettered by the old ways. To mock tradition, they wear masks made from Exoswarm heads and use the Shepherd's Staff as a weapon, and thus become a force of unrestrained chaos in Lahai-Roi.

Name: Flora Reindeer
Desc: A mechanical reindeer, grown from the Exostrider's own parts. They often help the Roya in plant foraging.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG .

The mechanical antlers on its head can be damaged by Counterattacks. Successfully breaking the antlers will reduce its attack power.

After scanning the data of past animals in Lahai-Roi, the Exostrider used its own parts to grow and proliferate mechanical reindeer. These Flora Reindeer are companions to the Roya and help the Roya in plant foraging.

Name: Mining Reindeer
Desc: A mechanical reindeer, grown from the Exostrider's own parts. They often help the Roya in ore mining.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG .

The Mining Reindeer's pulse beam can be deflected to deal damage to itself.

The mechanical antlers on its head can be damaged by Counterattacks. Successfully breaking the antlers will reduce its attack power.

After scanning the data of past animals in Lahai-Roi, the Exostrider used its own parts to grow and proliferate mechanical reindeer. These Mining Reindeer are companions to the Roya and help the Roya in ore mining.

Name: Ironhoof
Desc: A mechanical muskox, grown from the Exostrider's own parts. They often help the Roya in transporting loads.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

The mechanical horns on its head can be damaged by Counterattacks. Successfully breaking the horns will reduce its attack power and range.

When the Ironhoof is under Tune Rupture - Shifting and hit by Tune Break, it takes 30% more total DMG for 15s.

After scanning the data of past animals in Lahai-Roi, the Exostrider used its own parts to grow and proliferate mechanical muskox. Due to the Ironhoof's tough armor, which allows it to survive frigid temperatures on long journeys, the Roya often use them to transport heavy loads.

Name: Spacetrek Explorer
Desc: A large, fast-moving mech developed by the Spacetrek Collective for both transport and defense.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.

The Spacetrek Explorer has sturdy armor and exceptional mobility. It is equipped with an electromagnetic gun and has a shield generator that can extend protection to allies.

Hitting Spacetrek Explorer with Tune Break disables its shield generator, removing its active Shield and preventing new ones from generating.

In the face of the vast unknown lands in Lahai-Roi, the Spacetrek Collective developed the multi-purpose Spacetrek Explorer to serve in transport, storage, and defense roles. Its wheeled legs and heavy-duty tires allow it to race along vertical surfaces, making it suitable for both urban and off-road terrain. The onboard storage compartments are designed to support long-term scientific expeditions.

Name: Sabercat Reaver
Desc: A highly aggressive predatory machine that prowls the wilderness of Lahai-Roi, hunting any sign of movement.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Fusion DMG.

When Sabercat Reaver is under Tune Rupture - Shifting and hit by Tune Break, it takes additional DMG equal to 25% of its Max HP, triggered only once.

This predatory machine has a single purpose: to shred everything in its path. It prowls the wilderness of Lahai-Roi, hunting any sign of movement. With the relentless buzz of its tail-mounted chainsaw, it seeks to execute its targets with cruel efficiency.

Desc: A humanoid shell formed of black sludge. It takes its place among the prey, patiently awaiting the hunters it was made to devour.

Name: Iceglint Dancer
Desc: A graceful humanoid TD dressed in ice.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.

Draped in a flowing skirt of ice, this humanoid Tacet Discord moves with airy grace. The razor-sharp blades beneath its feet trace frozen arcs that mimic figure skating but deliver only demise. It senses every tremor across the ice and reads the world through the frozen mirror. From the reflections, it learned elegance, then turned that elegance into a weapon. What was once a noble art has, in its hands, become a quiet choreography of death.

Name: Shadow Stepper
Desc: A faceless humanoid Tacet Discord with a body that flows, splits, and coalesces like a living shadow.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Havoc DMG.

A faceless humanoid Tacet Discord with a body that flows, splits, and coalesces like a living shadow. By splintering its own frequency, it can generate a pocket of shadow realm to lie in wait. When its prey draws near, it will erupt from the darkness to strike from impossible angles.

Name: Kronablight
Desc: A mysterious raptor-shaped Exoswarm that seems to mimic an extinct ancient animal.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Electro DMG.
This enemy has a higher All-Attribute RES.
This enemy has the Rage Feather ability and three weaknesses.

After remaining in combat for a period of time, Kronablight enters the Rage Feather state, greatly enhancing its ATK.
Weakness I: A successful Counterattack can damage its crest and wings, weakening its attacks and reducing the number of ranged projectiles.
Weakness II: Tune Break can remove its Rage Feather state and reduce its ranged projectiles.
Special Weakness: When Kronablight is under Tune Rupture - Shifting, hitting it with Tune Break will greatly reduce its All-Attribute RES.

A powerful raptor-like Exoswarm, mimicking an ancient predator known as Kronaclaw.
In the distant past, these raptors once circled the skies of Lahai-Roi, their dominion unchallenged since the First Epoch.
Legend holds that the Kronaclaw was a creation of Sol, sharing the divine sight of the Eye of Sol and beholding all that lay above and below Lahai-Roi.
But as magnetic poles shifted and the landscape was reshaped, the Kronaclaw species had faded into near extinction. Their place was eventually taken by Kronablight, an Exoswarm born of the Exostrider.

Name: Kronaclaw
Desc: A powerful raptor believed to be extinct, now appearing within the Upphaf Life Chamber.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.
This enemy has a higher All-Attribute RES.

Name: Glommoth
Desc: Among the Exoswarm, none stand taller nor command greater reverence than these towering figures. They are seen as reliable guides in leading their kin back to the Exostrider.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Glacio DMG.
While in combat, Glommoth gains Frost Armor, which increases its All-Attribute RES.
The Frost Armor is removed when the Fusion Burst on it reaches 3 stacks.

Weakness: Breaking Glommoth's left tusk causes it to lose part of its skill set.

Among the Exoswarm, none stand taller nor command greater reverence than these towering figures. Shaped in the likeness of the long-extinct mammoth, they are seen as reliable guides in leading their kin back to the Exostrider.
Glommoth, the male, advances with ferocity, often carving paths through frozen earth with its long, jagged tusks.
While Glommotha, the female, moves with care, guiding and healing the Exoswarm with its trunk as they return along the paths to the Exostrider.

Name: Sigillum
Desc: A wordless black mech, lost and longing to return home.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Spectro DMG.
When hit by an Echo Skill, Sigillum gains 1 stack of Off Course, up to 5 stacks. Echoes with the same name can only trigger this effect once.
Each stack of Off Course makes it take 10% more total DMG. At 5 stacks, Sigillum takes an additional 30% total DMG.

Rage and tears. Suffering and joy. Sorrow and pain. All are condensed, along with her sigh, into a single golden frequency.
It gazes at the sky, lost in memories worn thin by the ages. The lost voyager is still searching for the way home.

Name: Nameless Explorer
Desc: An ancient remnant entombed under the permafrost, a prisoner by its own hand.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.

Resonators can inflict up to 2 additional stacks of Tune Strain - Interfered on the Nameless Explorer.
When the Nameless Explorer is under Tune Strain - Interfered and hit by Tune Break, it gains 1 additional stack of Tune Strain - Interfered.
Each stack of Tune Strain - Interfered makes it take 4% more total DMG.

The station fell like a dying star, burying the dream of its era beneath ice and soil.
The Explorer was one of those who dreamed, who once walked among the stars.
But now he hides in the dark, corrupted by the Void.
Before reason and strength slipped entirely from his grasp, he sealed himself away in the cabin.

Yet, all transcendence comes at a price. Those who embraced this creation have become thralls to their own design, leaving behind nothing but hollow shells.

Name: Voidworm
Desc:
A shadow looming in the sand and storm.
An abomination woven from tens of thousands of Exoswarm.
Listen—the void-star hums, and it is near.
DescOpen: A higher-dimensional being originated from Aleph-1, which shaped its body with Exoswarm devoured by the Void Storm and forged its spine with the Exostrider's remnants. To the inhabitants of Lahai-Roi, Voidworm and Void Storm are equally terrifying. Both are unpredictable natural disasters that devour everything in their paths.

Name: Reminiscence: Kronaclaw
Desc: A powerful raptor believed to be extinct, now appearing within the Upphaf Life Chamber.
DescOpen:
This enemy has a higher RES to Aero DMG.
This enemy has a higher All-Attribute RES.
This enemy has the Rage Feather ability and three weaknesses.

After remaining in combat for a period of time, Kronaclaw enters the Rage Feather state, greatly enhancing its ATK.
Weakness I: A successful Counterattack can damage its crest and wings, weakening its attacks and reducing the number of ranged projectiles.
Weakness II: Tune Break can remove its Rage Feather state and reduce its ranged projectiles.
Special Weakness: When Kronaclaw is under Tune Rupture - Shifting, hitting it with Tune Break will greatly reduce its All-Attribute RES.

An extinct ancient raptor reproduced through the Upphaf Life Chamber.
In the distant past, these raptors once circled the skies of Lahai-Roi, their dominion unchallenged since the First Epoch.
Legend holds that the Kronaclaw was a creation of Sol, sharing the divine sight of the Eye of Sol and beholding all that lay above and below Lahai-Roi.
But as magnetic poles shifted and the landscape was reshaped, the Kronaclaw species had faded into near extinction. Their place was eventually taken by Kronablight, an Exoswarm born of the Exostrider.
